title: Feat
description: A feat (2024 rules), in four categories: origin, general, fighting_style, epic_boon. Feats are an instance of the unified ability pattern — their mechanical effects are expressed as effect_primitives, mirroring feature.schema.json. See docs/srd/srd-coverage-audit.md §3 and stages-1-3-plan.md WS-A4.

id.description: Stable id; ft_ prefix + lowercase snake_case (e.g. ft_great_weapon_master).

category.description: Feat category (2024). Gates eligibility: origin = at character creation; general = level 4+ (typically); fighting_style = requires a Fighting Style feature; epic_boon = level 19+.

prerequisites.description: Eligibility gates. Empty/absent = no prerequisite. Validated by engine/creation.py (WS-A6), which separates rules_valid from engine_supported.

ability_scores.description: Minimum ability scores required (any-of unless all_required is true).

ability_scores_all_required.description: false = any one listed ability meeting `min` qualifies (e.g. 'Str or Dex 13+'); true = all must meet their min.

requires_feature.description: Feature ids required (e.g. a Fighting Style feature for fighting_style feats; Spellcasting for some general feats).

other.description: Free-text prerequisite facts that don't map to a structured gate (e.g. 'Spellcasting or Pact Magic feature').

repeatable.description: Whether the feat can be taken more than once (e.g. Ability Score Improvement).

ability_score_increase.description: The ASI option many feats grant. `points` total to distribute; `per_ability_max` caps points per ability (1 or 2); `from` restricts which abilities; `max_score` is the per-score ceiling (20, or 30 for some epic boons).

grants.description: Non-effect things a feat confers at acquisition: proficiencies, spells/cantrips (Magic Initiate), a fighting style, speeds, etc. References use the relevant content ids where modeled.

fighting_style.description: ft_* id of a fighting-style feat granted.

trigger.description: For feats whose effects fire on an event (e.g. an opportunity-attack-shaped feat). Mirrors feature.schema.json#trigger.

effect_primitives.description: The mechanical-effect hook — the engine-real part of the feat, expressed as primitives (e.g. attack_modifier, save_modifier, damage). Mirrors feature.schema.json. Feats with no modeled combat effect (Skilled, Tavern Brawler's social bits) omit this.

aliases.description: Alternate internal names for content resolution (WS-I import matching).

public_name.description: PI-safe public display name override (WS-I rename layer, plan §3.8).

stage4_stub.description: Namespaced Stage-4 extension blob (plan §3.10): non-combat / exploration / social facets of the feat. No engine semantics; speculative data only.
